text: 1 ACCOUNT ENQUIRY Dear Sir/Madam, Property Enquiry: 114 Mackie Street KENSINGTON WA 6151 (Assessment #74087) Parcel 270/P4908 In accordance with City of South Perth Town planning Scheme No. 6 a) Zoning; Residential b) Density Coding; R15 c) Building Height; 7.0m There are no outstanding requisitions or orders made or issued at this time. There are no orders or notices issued requiring work to any adjoining or abutting land at this time. The property is currently subject to City of South Perth Town Planning Scheme No. 6. The Building Line is currently as prescribed by City of South Perth Town Planning Scheme No. 6. Town Planning Scheme No 6 was gazetted on the 29th April 2003. There are no resolutions passed or considered for the resumption or compulsory acquisition of the whole or any part of the property or any material thereon at this time. OTHER PLANNING MATTERS Local Planning Policy P351.5 Streetscape Compatibility – Precinct 5 ‘Arlington’ and Precinct 6 ‘Kensington’ applies to this site. Local Planning Policies can be accessed on the City’s website at: https://southperth.wa.gov.au/development/planning/planning-policies. Any further enquiries, please contact our Planning Services Department on 9474 0777. 11 January 2022 HUNT SETTLEMENTS jodie@huntsettlements.com 2 21/22 RATES AND CHARGES LEVIED Please advise in writing by email if you do not intend to adjust on 2021/2022 Rates and Charges. Failure to do so could result in legal action being taken against purchaser for unpaid rates. If settlement is delayed or cancelled, please contact the City on 9474 0777. Brought forward arrears $0.00 Rates $2347.53 Emergency Services Levy $446.00 Rubbish Service Charge $325.00 Swimming Pool Inspection Charge $0.00 Penalty Interest / Instalment Admin $0.00 Underground Power Network Charge $0.00 Underground Power Connection Fee $0.00 Less State Government Rebate -$111.50 Less payments -$1453.52 BALANCE OUTSTANDING AS AT 11 January 2022 $1553.51 UNDERGROUND POWER Service charge not applicable to the property Please contact the City on 9474 0777 to obtain an up to date balance for Settlement; penalty interest and other charges may be applicable. Please find below BPAY Payment Details Biller Code: 192294 Ref: 0000 0074 0871 LIABILITY FOR PAYMENT Regarding liability for payment of Rates and Charges (incl Underground Power) please liaise with your Settlement Agent. It is the owners’ responsibility to make sure that any building or structure that requires building approval is obtained from the City of South Perth. Any unauthorised building work that requires retrospective approval by the City of South Perth shall be cleared before settlement occurs, unless agreed in writing with the City Senior Building Surveyor. This includes any non-compliant Swimming Pool Safety Barrier. 3 If a non-complaint swimming pool safety barrier has been inspected by the City and was found to be non- compliant at the time of inspection, the Owner/s are required to contact the City to arrange a suitable time for re-inspection and clearance of the outstanding matters. Any further enquiries, please contact our Building Services Department on 9474 0777. Settlements Agents and Real Estate Agents may be reported to the relevant Authorities and/or to their Professional Associations in case of non-compliance with the City’s requests. Note: Non-Compliant swimming pool/spa safety barriers do not restrict access by young children to the swimming pool/spa and its immediate surrounds and therefore is deemed to be unsafe. Penalty for this sub regulation: a fine of $5 000 (Building Regulations (WA) 2012 - Reg. 50(1). (a) A site inspection has not been carried out to confirm the information forwarded. (b) Notices Issued: No (c) Notice Type: (d) Has a Building Licence been issued in the last 7 years: No (e) Home Indemnity Policy No: (f) Swimming Pool: No (g) New swimming pool – first inspection required: (h) Outstanding four year inspection: (i) Pool compliance: Any further enquiries, please contact our Building Services Department on 9474 0777. ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH SERVICES No INFRASTRUCTURE SERVICES Works abutting this property are unknown at this stage. Any further enquiries please contact our Infrastructure Services Department on 9474 0777. Yours faithfully S Garwood Suzette Garwood FINANCE OFFICER 4 SCHEDULE 1. a) Zoning. b) Density Coding c) Building Height Limit 2. Outstanding requisitions or orders made or issued. 3. Orders or notices issued requiring work to any adjoining or abutting land. 4. Schemes; orders or plans placing restrictions upon or in any way limiting any building thereon. 5. Has any building line been fixed? 6. Resolutions passed or schemes prepared in relation to Town Planning which are or may affect the property and have come into operation? 7. Resolutions passed or considered for the resumption or compulsory acquisition of the whole or any part of the property or any material thereon. 8. All rates and charges;- a) Arrears Unpaid b) Current Paid c) Current Unpaid 9. Special matters considered advisable to be known to the purchases of the premises. 5 PLANNING SCHEDULE - ACCOUNT ENQUIRIES The following planning information is provided on all account enquiries: 1. City of South Perth Town Planning Scheme No. 6 (TPS6) Zoning.* 2. TPS6 Density Coding.* 4. TPS6 Building Height Limit.* 8. Council Planning Policies – Residential design guidelines for proposed developments that apply to this site (only 1 of the following policies will be listed): o P302 - General Design Guidelines for Residential Development. o P351.5 - Streetscape Compatibility - Precinct 5 ‘Arlington’ and Precinct 6 ‘Kensington’. o P351.14 - Cygnia Cove Residential Design Guidelines. *For properties located in Special Control Area SCA1, the TPS6 zoning, density coding and building height limit shown on the Scheme Maps are only applicable to minor additions/alterations to existing developments approved prior to 18 January 2013. *For properties located in the Centre zone, refer to the Canning Bridge Activity Centre Plan. The following planning information is only provided on account enquiries if it is applicable: 3. A MRS Regional Road Reservation applies to this site. 5. The site is located in a precinct/Special Control Area where specific development requirements apply for new development: o Precinct 15: South Perth Station (Special Control Area SCA1). o Canning Bridge Activity Centre. 6. The special setbacks from specified streets apply to this site (TPS6 Tables 2 and 5). 7. The site is listed in the Municipal Heritage Inventory. 9. There are significant trees on this site. 10. There are active planning compliance investigations for this site. 11. The site is located within a precinct that may be subject to a future change in planning controls: o Canning Highway (#ShapeOurPlace). o Waterford Triangle. 12. The site is opposite the proposed Manning Community Hub. Information Notes: 1-8. The City of South Perth Town Planning Scheme No. 6, Town Planning Policies and the Municipal Heritage Inventory are available at no charge from the City’s website at: http://www.southperth.wa.gov.au/development. 5. Precinct 15: South Perth Station (Special Control Area SCA1) - The provisions of TPS6 Schedule 9A of apply to all comprehensive new development located within Special Control Area SCA1. 5. Canning Bridge Activity Centre - The provisions of the Canning Bridge Activity Centre Plan (Version 3 approved by WAPC April 2016) apply to all new development located within the Canning Bridge Activity Centre 11. Canning Highway (#ShapeOurPlace) - This area is under consideration for future changes, including increased building height limits and/or design guidelines to apply to new development. Further information may be accessed on the City's website at https://southperth.wa.gov.au/our-future/projects-and-places/canning-highway. 11. Waterford Triangle - This area is identified for future changes, including intensification of use through increased residential density and building height, and improvements to public infrastructure. Further information may be accessed on the City's website at https://southperth.wa.gov.au/our-future/projects-and-places/waterford-triangle. 12. Manning Community Hub - The Manning Community Hub project comprises the Manning Community Centre and the future expansion of commercial activities. Further information may be accessed on the City's website at https://southperth.wa.gov.au/our-future/projects-and-places/manning-community-hub. 6 13. Bushfire Prone Area - This property is located wholly within a Bushfire Prone Area in accordance with the order issued by the Fire and Emergency Services Commissioner. As a result, additional planning and building requirements may apply to your future development. For detailed information please contact the City’s Building and Planning Services Departments. Important Information: The City of South Perth provides information about properties in the District as a service to the Community. This information is a general overview of options that may apply under the City’s Town Planning Scheme. The City is not in a position to make a definitive assessment on the development potential of a property without the submission of a detailed planning application and proposal. You should carefully evaluate this information and determine if this information is adequate for the purposes that you intend to use it for. This may require you to assess the information in more detail, in the context of the specifics of the property, the applicable Local Laws, Planning Schemes and other relevant documents. You should also consider whether to obtain independent professional advice. The City of South Perth disclaims all liability to any person whatsoever, for any loss sustained in relation to anything done in purported reliance of this information.
